page: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU bundled with this instance. Network performance is anchored by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read-only operations reaching 586.09 GB/sec. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to top-tier performance in Passmark CPU extended instructions and cryptography tests. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in Redis SET operations, handling 270.56 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 268.34 GB/sec.

Given its compute-heavy profile and high vCPU density relative to memory, the c8i.48xlarge offers high resource density for CPU-bound workloads without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. It is highly suited for compute-intensive tasks such as large-scale software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, with no local storage or GPU. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It is highly optimized for compute-intensive workloads, including multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, high-throughput Redis databases, and medium-model LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86 platform featuring 192 vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded and cryptographic workloads.

384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
